title: rhynchosinapis cheiranthos subsp. cheiranthiflora
intro: Rhynchosinapis cheiranthos subsp. cheiranthiflora, also known as Horned mustard, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is native to Europe and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, improving respiratory issues, and supporting digestive health. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
